summary: Close the literal AC-11.a mutation denominator with ordered, durable, application-owned undo and redo.

decisions.topic: Complete application-owned command ordering and compensation
decisions.decision: Reserve command-history slots at dispatch time, publish frames and entries only in dispatch order, make undo and redo exclusive against later mutations, and hold later non-project mutations behind pending project transitions while retaining concurrent project winner/discard and recovery-staleness behavior. Store semantic or durable inverses for documents, project files, annotations, parameters, settings, layout, viewer, console, Welcome, and MCP controls; keep render and cancel concurrent.
decisions.rationale: Completion-order frame publication can undo an earlier slow command before a later edit, and broad project snapshots can otherwise absorb or erase later work. Ordered slots plus clean project-transition barriers preserve per-action chronology without delaying ordinary optimistic UI updates or preventing render cancellation.

testEvidence.criterion: Every applicable mutating UI action produces exactly one chronological history entry whose undo and redo restore only that action, including slow settings saves, project-file operations, persisted annotations, external-change choices, console clearing, Welcome preference, and MCP controls.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused AC-11.a runtime/UI bundle once, repair only observed defects, then rerun the affected runtime/project files and strict static gates.

red.command: pnpm.cmd vitest run tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime-history.test.ts --reporter=dot; independent AC-11.a denominator reviews
red.outcome: RED: the restart-level annotation-path test failed 1 of 15; later independent reviews found nonundoable external-change, console, Welcome and MCP mutations, then found completion-order races and redo contamination between a deferred project mutation and a later edit. One intermediate focused bundle also deterministically exposed 15 render/cancel regressions from an overbroad queue; two existing project atomicity tests rejected an overexclusive correction.

green.command: pnpm.cmd vitest run tests/application/diagnostics/console-state.test.ts tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime-history.test.ts tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime-layout.test.ts tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime-projects.test.ts tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime.test.ts tests/application/viewer/annotation-persistence.test.ts tests/app/App.test.tsx tests/ui/Workbench.editor-settings.test.tsx tests/ui/mcp/use-mcp-stdio.test.tsx tests/ui/viewer/ViewerPaneConnector.test.tsx tests/ui/welcome/WelcomeLauncher.test.tsx --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd vitest run tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime-history.test.ts tests/application/runtime/workbench-runtime-projects.test.ts --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd typecheck; git diff --check
green.outcome: GREEN: the complete focused bundle passed 11 files/123 tests; after the final clean-project-redo barrier, the affected history and project set passed 2 files/36 tests. Biome/source policy checked 468 files with both capped UI files at 400 lines, TypeScript exited 0, diff hygiene passed, and the final independent denominator review returned literal 0 blocker / 0 critical / 0 major / 0 minor / 0 nit.

nearMisses.sought: Adversarial chronology, persistence, and concurrency review across the literal AC-11.a mutation denominator
nearMisses.confirmation: Successive reviews caught stale source replay, parameter revision drift, incorrect replay quality, inverse-publication ordering, missing project filesystem compensation, async frame races, annotation path loss, bypassed UI settings, console concurrency, completion-order undo, and project redo contamination. Each actionable finding received a bounded regression before the final 0/0/0/0/0 verdict.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
